pi_items.source: Verified via Tridium official website; Tridium JACE 8000 is a real building management controller product with Niagara 4 framework. Web search: https://www.tridium.com/us/en/Products/niagara/jace
Setup_task.sh line 18-29 explicitly defines ownership criterion. Task seeds assets with both 'Owned' and 'Leased' statuses to test filtering capability.
Setup_task.sh line 19, 28 defines status criterion. Task seeds assets with both 'Active' and 'Retired' statuses to test filtering capability.

Web search for CVE-2026-9912 in NVD, CVE.mitre.org, CVEDetails, and general search yields NO results. This CVE identifier does not exist in any public CVE database as of March 2026. Search results show CVE-2019-8998, CVE-2019-13528, CVE-2025-3943, and CVE-2025-3944 for Tridium vulnerabilities, but NOT CVE-2026-9912. Web searches: https://nvd.nist.gov/, https://cve.mitre.org/, https://www.cvedetails.com/

Security bulletin created at /home/ga/Desktop/security_bulletin_cve_2026.txt in setup_task.sh line 15-39 specifies: 'severity: CRITICAL' and 'Priority: Critical / High'. Metadata value 'Critical' matches the bulletin.
Security bulletin created in setup_task.sh line 36 specifies: 'Due Date: 2026-06-15'. Exact match with metadata value.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic data with 6 seeded BMS controllers (CTRL-J8-001 through CTRL-J8-004, CTRL-J8-003, CTRL-J8-OLD), of which 3 are eligible (model=Tridium JACE 8000, ownership=Owned, status=Active). The task requires creating firmware upgrade work orders with description containing 'CVE-2026-9912', priority 'Critical', and planned date '2026-06-15'. CRITICAL FINDING: CVE-2026-9912 does not exist in any public CVE database; this is a fabricated vulnerability identifier used only within this synthetic task environment. The security bulletin file and all task parameters are created dynamically during task setup.
